core_mechanism: representing a conditional predictive distribution as a finite weighted mixture of Gaussian components (a mixture-density model) fitted by optimizing a proper scoring rule, so that heavy tails and multimodality are captured beyond what a single unimodal density allows
brief_reason: The abstract's central comparison is between a point head, a single-Gaussian density head, and a K=4 Gaussian-mixture density head, with the mixture found to capture tail risk (via CRPS/pinball proper scoring) beyond a unimodal Gaussian, especially in high-volatility regimes.
